messages.text: I'm getting a CORS error when my React frontend hits the Flask API on localhost:5000.
You need flask-cors. Install it and add CORS(app) to your Flask app.
That fixed it. Thanks!
TypeScript is inferring `any` for my generic function and I can't figure out why.
Can you share the function signature? Often the issue is missing the type parameter in the return type.
You're right — I had `function wrap<T>(x): T[]` instead of `function wrap<T>(x: T): T[]`. Adding the type on the parameter fixed the inference.
My Postgres query over the events table is taking 12 seconds. It's a simple WHERE on user_id and created_at.
Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE — you likely need a composite index on (user_id, created_at DESC).
Added the composite index and it's down to 40ms. Huge improvement.
My Docker container can't reach my host's Postgres on localhost:5432.
Inside the container, localhost is the container itself. Use `host.docker.internal` on Mac/Windows or the host's gateway IP on Linux.
Switched to host.docker.internal and it connects fine now.
My useEffect is firing infinitely. It sets state which triggers a re-render which re-runs the effect.
You need a proper dependency array. If you're setting state based on props, put the prop in the array — not the state you're setting.
Right, I had an object literal in the deps. Moved it outside or used useMemo and the loop stopped.
My Kubernetes pod keeps CrashLoopBackOff. Logs show 'connection refused' for the database.
Check if the service exists and the pod has the right env vars. Also look at the readiness probe — the DB might not be ready when the pod starts.
The readiness probe was the issue. Added an initial delay and the pod stabilized.
My Python virtualenv is broken. pip can't find any packages after I moved the project folder.
Virtualenvs hardcode the interpreter path. Delete the venv and recreate it: `rm -rf .venv && python -m venv .venv`.
Recreated it and reinstalled from requirements.txt. Everything works now.
I have a git merge conflict in package-lock.json and I can't resolve it manually.
Don't try to hand-resolve lockfile conflicts. Delete the file, run `npm install` again, and commit the regenerated lockfile.
Worked perfectly. npm regenerated a clean lockfile.
Our prod TLS cert expired overnight. Now customers can't hit the API.
Get Let's Encrypt via certbot immediately: `certbot renew --force-renewal`. Set up auto-renewal via cron afterward.
Renewed and configured the cron. Also added a Datadog monitor for cert expiry 14 days out.
Should I enable SQLite WAL mode for a mostly-read workload with occasional writes?
Yes — WAL gives you readers that don't block on writers. `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL` once, and it persists.
Enabled it. Read latency under concurrent writes dropped noticeably.
How do I refresh an OAuth token when it expires mid-request?
Wrap your fetch in a 401-retry handler: on 401, call the refresh endpoint with the refresh token, store the new access token, and retry the original request once.
Implemented the retry wrapper with a mutex so concurrent requests don't all try to refresh at once. Clean.
We're adding a new content-type to our GraphQL schema. Is it better to make it a union or an interface?
Use an interface if all variants share common fields. Use a union when they have nothing in common. Interfaces play nicer with fragment inlining on the client.
Went with interface since we share id, createdAt, and authorId across types.
Our webpack bundle is 4.2MB. How do I get it under 1MB?
Start with bundle-analyzer to find the biggest culprits. Tree-shake lodash by importing from `lodash/get` directly, and dynamic-import your heavy routes.
Lodash was the big win — dropped 600KB. Dynamic imports on the admin panel shaved another 900KB.
I'm setting up NGINX as a reverse proxy in front of a Node.js app. Websockets aren't connecting.
NGINX needs the Upgrade and Connection headers for websockets. Add `proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade` and `proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade'` to your location block.
Added those headers and the websocket handshake completes now.
Our Redis cache is returning stale data after a database write. When should I invalidate?
Invalidate inside the same transaction as the write. Write to DB, then DEL the key before the transaction commits. Cache-aside pattern only reads from DB on cache miss.
Moved the DEL inside the transaction wrapper. No more staleness.
My Lambda is getting AccessDenied when reading from an S3 bucket. Both are in the same account.
Check the Lambda execution role — it needs `s3:GetObject` on the specific bucket ARN. And make sure the bucket policy doesn't explicitly deny it.
The role was missing the resource ARN. Added it and the reads work now.
Our Node.js service memory grows from 200MB to 2GB over 12 hours. I can't find the leak.
Take heap snapshots 1h apart and compare them in Chrome DevTools. Look for retained objects that shouldn't persist — event listeners and closures over large objects are common culprits.
Found it — we had a global event emitter with listeners added per request but never removed. Fixed the cleanup and memory is stable now.
Vite HMR stopped working after I added a Docker dev setup. Changes don't show up in the browser.
Inside Docker, Vite's HMR websocket needs to know the external host. Set `server.hmr.host` and `server.hmr.clientPort` in vite.config.ts, and use `server.watch.usePolling = true` for fsevents.
Added polling and the HMR host config. Hot reload works inside the container now.
Prisma migration failed mid-run in production. The schema is now out of sync with the database.

First, run `prisma migrate resolve --rolled-back <migration-name>` to mark it failed. Then fix the SQL manually or write a new migration that handles the partial state before re-running migrate deploy.
Marked it rolled back and wrote a corrective migration. Prod is stable again.
The Rust compiler is yelling at me about a lifetime — 'borrowed value does not live long enough'.
You're probably returning a reference to a local variable. Either return the owned value (use `String` not `&str`) or make the caller pass in the buffer.
Changed the function to return `String` and the borrow checker is happy.
Two Go goroutines are deadlocking — each is holding a mutex the other needs.
Classic AB-BA deadlock. Establish a total ordering on lock acquisition — every goroutine must acquire mutex A before mutex B, never the reverse.
Refactored to always acquire in the same order and the deadlock is gone.
Our GitHub Actions pipeline is failing in CI but the same tests pass locally.
Likely an env var or filesystem case-sensitivity difference. Check that your imports use the exact filename casing and that CI secrets are set for the workflow.
It was a case issue — `./Utils.ts` vs `./utils.ts`. Mac is case-insensitive, Linux in CI isn't. Fixed the imports.
Tailwind classes are missing from my production build even though they work in dev.
Tailwind's purge only scans files listed in your content config. Make sure all your template paths are in `content: [...]` in tailwind.config.js, including dynamic class names.
Added the missing paths. Classes show up in prod now.
I have a Supabase RLS policy that should allow users to read their own rows, but queries return empty.
Check that the policy uses `auth.uid() = user_id` and that your client passes the JWT. Without the auth header, the row filter sees no user and returns nothing.
The client was using the anon key without the bearer token. Added the session and rows come back correctly.
My FFmpeg h264 encode is producing huge files at decent quality. What flags give better compression?
Use `-c:v libx264 -crf 22 -preset slow`. CRF 22 is visually lossless for most content, and `slow` trades encode time for better compression.
CRF 22 with preset slow gave me a 40% smaller file at the same visual quality. Exactly what I needed.
macOS code signing is failing with 'errSecInternalComponent' when I try to notarize my app.

That usually means the keychain is locked or the signing identity isn't accessible to the build process. Run `security unlock-keychain` before codesign and verify the identity with `security find-identity -v`.
Unlocking the keychain fixed it. Notarization passes now.
I need to group a MongoDB collection by date and compute averages. How do I write the aggregation?
Use `$group` with `_id: { $dateToString: { format: '%Y-%m-%d', date: '$createdAt' } }` and `$avg` for the numeric field. Follow with `$sort` on _id if you want chronological output.
That pipeline gives me exactly the daily averages I needed. Thanks.
We're debating how to version our REST API. Should we use URL paths like /v1/ or headers?
URL path versioning is more discoverable and easier to cache. Header versioning is cleaner in theory but harder to test with curl and breaks CDN caching. Most teams go with /v1/ in the path.
Agreed, we'll use /v1/ in the path. Simpler for clients.
I'm migrating my Chrome extension from Manifest V2 to V3. Background scripts are breaking.

V3 replaces persistent background pages with service workers. You can't use `window` or `document` — rewrite as an event-driven service worker and use `chrome.storage` for state instead of module globals.
Converted the background script to a service worker and moved state into chrome.storage.local. Extension loads cleanly in V3.
Someone on my team accidentally committed a secret. What's the safest way to purge it from git history?

Use `git filter-repo` to remove the file from history, then force-push. Critically — rotate the secret immediately since it was public, and add a GitHub Actions secret-scanning check so this doesn't happen again.
Rotated the key, filter-repo'd the history, and set up gitleaks in CI. Good prevention for next time.
